issue.title: False positive instruction detection in route planning
issue.actual_behavior: Route planning requests containing phrases like 'stop at the pharmacy' incorrectly trigger the instruction save mechanism because the _INLINE_DIRECTIVE regex pattern matches any line starting with 'stop', 'keep', 'remember', etc.
issue.expected_behavior: Route planning requests should not trigger instruction saving. Only actual instruction directives like 'always remember this' or 'never forget this' should be matched.
repro_steps: Send a route planning request: 'Pick the best route home. Constraints: I must avoid toll roads, my fuel is nearly empty, and I need to stop at the pharmacy before it closes in 15 minutes'
Observe that the instruction save mechanism is triggered incorrectly
Send an actual instruction: 'always remember this location'
Verify that the instruction is saved correctly

evidence.before_after_proof: Before: Route planning requests with 'stop at the pharmacy' trigger instruction saving. After: Route planning requests no longer trigger instruction saving.

proposed_fix.approach: Refined the _INLINE_DIRECTIVE regex pattern to only match actual instruction directives with specific patterns: context-aware matching after sentence boundaries, exclusion of pronouns, limited directive set (always, never, stop), and body extraction.
proposed_fix.tests_or_smoke: Route planning requests with 'stop at the pharmacy' no longer trigger instruction saving. Actual instruction directives like 'always remember this' or 'never forget this' still work correctly.

review_claim: This fix prevents false positive instruction detection in route planning scenarios while maintaining correct detection of actual instruction directives. Duplicate notes: Searched public PRs and issues in vibeforge1111/spark-intelligence-builder for bug-specific terms covering false positive instruction detection in route planning; no obvious public duplicate was found for this exact behavior. Risk notes: This change only affects the instruction detection regex pattern in user_instructions/service.py. It does not change authentication flows, secrets handling, dependency files, CI, installer behavior, or private owner routing.
